response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable full-stack software craftsman with a broad polyglot background, but the resume reveals a fundamental mismatch with the role's core requirements: C++ is essentially absent from her skill set (only a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project), falling far short of the mandatory 8 years; her infrastructure work is incidental rather than a primary focus; and she has no cloud compute platform, storage architecture, or distributed systems leadership experience at the depth this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to deliver production systems across web, mobile, embedded, and geo-data domains, showing genuine breadth and adaptability
Active community contributor with public workshops, conference talks, and maintained open-source projects, indicating a genuine mentoring and knowledge-sharing disposition
Experience at Sociomantic working on micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second shows some exposure to high-throughput distributed infrastructure
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately 6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — against a mandatory minimum of 8 years; this alone disqualifies the application
No cloud architecture experience: AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is build-tooling level, not production cloud compute platform design or ownership
No formal technical leadership in infrastructure, platform engineering, or DevOps; leadership evidence is limited to community workshops and open-source maintainership
